rationale:
**AC1 BLOCK:** `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/providers/+page.svelte:onMount` performs a single GraphQL request and never polls, subscribes, or re-queries after `refreshProviderStatuses` completes, so rows cannot patch live within 5s of probe completion.
**AC1 BLOCK:** `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/providers/+page.svelte:63` keeps `defaultRouteStatus` in the initial page query, and `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_providers.go:102` still resolves it synchronously, so the slow-load path is not fully removed.
**AC2 BLOCK:** `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_providers.go:435` only maps `HarnessInfo.Quota`; `QuotaFromRateLimitSignal` is explicitly “for future call sites” at `resolver_providers.go:488`, so Claude/Codex parsed headers do not populate harness quota rows.
**AC2 BLOCK:** `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/providers/+page.svelte:350` renders only utilization or “not reported”; there is no 7-day sparkline in the list row when hourly buckets are available.
**AC2 BLOCK:** `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_providers.go:412` always returns a non-null usage object with zero-valued pointer fields, so missing usage data renders as `0 / 0` instead of null/`not reported`.
**AC3 REQUEST_CHANGES:** `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/providers/[name]/+page.svelte:154` renders the quota ceiling as text below the chart, not as an overlay on the 7-day/30-day time series.
**AC3 REQUEST_CHANGES:** `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/providers/agent-endpoints.e2e.ts:137` mocks `providerTrend`; there is no Playwright or server fixture proving seeded ascending usage plus a real quota produces the projection.
**AC4 BLOCK:** No provider-specific perf test or fixture was added under `cli/internal/server/graphql` or `cli/internal/server/perf` for ≥1,000 rows, ≥5 endpoints/harnesses, unified-list p95 ≤200ms, trend-detail p95 ≤400ms, or 10,000-row stretch behavior.
